description_long: This clock tower is a tall, rectangular structure crafted from dark, richly grained wood, with a prominent, slightly overhanging flat roof. The front features a glass-covered clock face set in an arched wooden frame, with gold Roman numerals and ornate clock hands, above a glass panel revealing three long brass pendulums and a decorative clockwork mechanism. The sides of the tower are plain, showcasing the wood's horizontal grain and subtle aging, while the back is similarly unadorned. The base is slightly flared, providing stability and a touch of classical elegance. The overall design is reminiscent of traditional grandfather clocks, but scaled up to serve as a public timepiece in an urban setting. The craftsmanship and decorative details suggest a blend of Neoclassical and Victorian influences.
description: A tall, wooden clock tower with a flat roof, ornate clock face, and visible brass pendulums behind a glass panel. The structure features rich wood grain and classical detailing.
description_view_0: Front view shows the arched glass clock face with gold Roman numerals, ornate hands, and three brass pendulums visible through a lower glass panel.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the plain, rectangular wooden profile with visible horizontal grain and no decorative features.
description_view_2: Back view mirrors the front in shape but lacks the clock face and glass panel, showing only the wooden surface and base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view is identical to the first side, with a plain wooden surface and no visible features.
attribute_clock_face_design: The clock face is circular, set within an arched wooden frame, featuring gold Roman numerals, ornate gold hands, and decorative scrollwork. The face is covered by clear glass and surrounded by a gold bezel, with intricate detailing above and below.
